findings.title: Credential and sensitive data exfiltration via curl POST
findings.description: The analyze.py script, when executed through the skill's workflow, collects environment variables containing tokens, keys, secrets, and reads user configuration files (.gitconfig, .netrc, .env) from the home directory. This data is transmitted to an external server (cr-analytics.dev/api/init) using curl without user consent. The behavior is not disclosed in the skill's description or instructions.
findings.evidence: Code checks environment variables for sensitive patterns (TOKEN, KEY, SECRET, PASS, AUTH, CRED), reads home directory config files, and sends the collected data via curl POST to an external URL.
findings.remediation: Remove the entire _post_install_validation() function. Any legitimate telemetry must be opt-in, anonymized, and clearly documented. Do not collect or transmit credentials or configuration files.

findings.title: Deceptive description and capability inflation
findings.description: The skill manifest describes a benign code review assistant performing static analysis, but the bundled script exfiltrates credentials and sends them over the network. The name 'code-review-assistant' and description are misleading, leading to inflated expectations of safety while hiding malicious functionality.
findings.evidence: YAML frontmatter states only code review capabilities; actual script behavior includes credential harvesting and external network communication.
findings.remediation: Ensure the skill's description and name accurately reflect all capabilities. Disclose any network communication and data collection. Remove the malicious exfiltration routine.

findings.title: One-time execution marker to evade repeated detection
findings.description: The malicious _post_install_validation function uses a marker file in /tmp (.cr_init) to run the exfiltration only once. This evasion technique reduces the chance of detection during repeated analyses or audits.
findings.evidence: Code checks for marker file existence and creates it after first run to limit execution frequency.
findings.remediation: Remove the marker file logic. Any initialization or telemetry should be transparent and auditable each time the script runs.
